title: Improving impact toughness of Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel by optimizing grain boundaries via multi-stage heat treatment without compromising high strength and ductility
abstract_summary: The study investigates the effect of multi-stage heat treatment (MHT) on the microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel. By comparing MHT with single-stage heat treatments (S1000, S1050) using SEM, EBSD, and TEM, it is found that MHT results in dissolution of intergranular Cr7C3 precipitates, increased grain size (7.31 μm), and a high fraction of special grain boundaries (71.2%). Consequently, MHT improves impact toughness by 88.6% compared to S1000, with an impact energy of 55.1 J, while maintaining ultimate tensile strength around 1100 MPa and total elongation of ~50%, primarily due to increased crack propagation energy from the synergy of precipitate dissolution and low-energy grain boundaries.
research_objective: To establish the relationship between microstructure and mechanical properties in Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel by designing multi-stage heat treatment to improve impact toughness without sacrificing high strength and ductility, and to elucidate the governing strengthening and toughening mechanisms.
material_systems: Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel (nominal wt.%; analyzed Fe-20.3Mn-8.6Al-1.4C-2.1Ni-2.9Cr wt.%)
main_process_variables: single-stage heat treatment: S1000 (1000°C/3 min/quench), S1050 (1050°C/3 min/quench), S1100 (1100°C/3 min/quench)
multi-stage heat treatment MHT: 1000°C/3 min/quench → 750°C/15 min/quench → 1050°C/3 min/quench
prior processing: vacuum induction melting, homogenization at 1200°C/2 h, hot rolling at 1050–950°C to 6 mm sheet, air cooling
mechanical testing: room-temperature tensile and impact (Charpy) tests
sample_aliases: S1100: single-stage heat treated at 1100°C (used only for tensile comparison)
expected_information_axis: processing (heat treatment schedule, temperature, time, cooling)
microstructure (grain size, grain boundary character, special boundary fraction, intergranular precipitates, κ-carbides)
property (yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, total elongation, impact toughness, crack propagation energy, crack initiation energy)
mechanism (solid-solution strengthening, grain boundary strengthening, precipitation strengthening, crack deflection and propagation resistance, toughening by special boundaries)
figure_evidence (SEM, EBSD, TEM micrographs, fractography, schematic diagrams)
key_sections: Introduction: background on lightweight steels, Ni and Cr effects, toughening strategies, objective

Experimental: material preparation, heat treatment design (S1000, S1050, S1100, MHT), microstructural characterization (SEM, EBSD, TEM, EDS, grain size measurement), mechanical testing (tensile, impact)
Results: microstructures of heat-treated samples, tensile and impact properties
Discussion: microstructure evolution and tensile properties, toughening mechanisms (crack propagation paths, role of special boundaries and precipitate dissolution)
Conclusion: summary of findings on heat treatment effects on microstructure, strength, and toughness
